name: LLMRouter
tagline: Open-source library and CLI for cost-aware LLM routing with 16+ router algorithms
metaDescription: LLMRouter is an open-source Python library and CLI that routes each query to the best LLM by cost and task, with 16+ routing algorithms and a plugin workflow.
overview: LLMRouter is an open-source Python library and command-line tool that picks the most suitable language model for each incoming query. Instead of sending every request to one model, it routes based on task complexity, cost, and performance, so you can use cheaper models for easy queries and stronger ones where they're needed.

It ships with more than 16 routing models, grouped into single-round, multi-round, agentic, and personalized routers. These cover strategies such as KNN, SVM, MLP, matrix factorization, Elo rating, graph-based, and BERT-based routing. There's also a data-generation pipeline that builds training data from 11 benchmark datasets with automatic API calling and evaluation.

As an LLM gateway, it sits between your application and several model providers. A unified `llmrouter` CLI handles training, inference, and an interactive Gradio chat UI, and a plugin workflow lets you add your own routers and tasks. It's aimed at developers and researchers who want to compare routing strategies or cut inference cost without locking into a single model.
features: Routes each query to a suitable model based on task complexity, cost, and performance
Over 16 router models across single-round, multi-round, agentic, and personalized categories
Unified `llmrouter` CLI for training, inference, and interactive chat
Gradio-based chat UI for trying routers interactively
Data-generation pipeline using 11 benchmark datasets with automatic API calling and evaluation
Plugin workflow for building custom routers and adding your own tasks

gettingStarted.intro: LLMRouter is published on PyPI as `llmrouter-lib` and exposes a unified `llmrouter` CLI. Install it, then use the CLI to train a router, run inference, or open the interactive chat UI.

steps.heading: Install from PyPI
steps.body: Install the library with pip. LLMRouter targets Python 3.10.

steps.heading: Explore the CLI
steps.body: After installing, the `llmrouter` command is available for training, inference, and the interactive chat interface. Run it with --help to see available subcommands.

steps.heading: Follow the docs for routing config
steps.body: The training and inference commands depend on your dataset, chosen router (for example knnrouter or mlprouter), and provider keys, so follow the project's online docs and per-router tutorials for exact arguments.

useCases: Cut LLM inference cost by sending simple queries to cheaper models and hard ones to stronger models
Benchmark and compare different routing strategies on standard datasets before production
Add a custom router or task for a domain-specific routing policy via the plugin workflow
Stand up an interactive chat UI to test how a trained router behaves across queries
